candidate_problems.text: How can 'specific content is load-bearing across counterfactual variation' be made precise and non-circular? The conjecture needs an operational criterion for when changing what a state is about genuinely forces coordinated changes in downstream inference, control, and error-correction, rather than merely redescribing causal roles after the fact. Without this, the view risks collapsing into an intuitive label rather than a testable explanatory distinction.

What exactly distinguishes genuine misrepresentation from mere malfunction or noise in physical systems? The conjecture treats error-detecting revision as crucial, but it remains open how to separate a system that represents falsely from one that is simply broken, underspecified, or statistically unreliable. A principled account is needed of normativity: where the standards of better and worse mapping come from within the system rather than from an external interpreter.

Where is the boundary between thin intentionality and non-intentional adaptive control, and does the proposed gradualism admit a principled threshold? Many systems—thermostats, homeostatic circuits, model-free controllers, predictive processing architectures, and learned world models—seem to occupy intermediate cases. The open question is which structural features are sufficient for intentionality, which are merely enabling, and whether the conjecture can classify borderline systems without arbitrariness.
